name: Triphala Churna
ingredients: Haritaki (Terminalia chebula), Bibhitaki (Terminalia bellirica), Amalaki (Emblica officinalis) in equal parts.
indications: Mild laxative (Anulomana), Eye health (Chakshushya), Rejuvenative (Rasayana), Constipation (Vibandha).

name: Trikatu Churna
ingredients: Shunthi (Zingiber officinale), Pippali (Piper longum), Maricha (Piper nigrum).
indications: Indigestion (Ajeerna), Low digestive fire (Mandagni), Cough & Cold (Kasa, Pratishyaya), Obesity (Sthaulya).

name: Sitopaladi Churna
ingredients: Sitopala (Rock Sugar), Vamshalochana (Bamboo manna), Pippali (Piper longum), Ela (Elettaria cardamomum), Tvak (Cinnamomum zeylanicum).
indications: Kasa (Cough), Shwasa (Asthma), Jwara (Fever), Kshaya (Tuberculosis), Mandagni (Low digestive fire).

name: Hingvashtak Churna
ingredients: Shunthi, Maricha, Pippali, Ajamoda, Saindhava Lavana, Shveta Jeeraka, Krishna Jeeraka, Shuddha Hingu.
indications: Abdominal bloating (Adhmana), Gas (Anaha), Indigestion (Agnimandya), Abdominal Pain (Shula). Primarily a Vata pacifier.
anupana: Warm water or first morsel of food with ghee.

name: Avipattikar Churna
ingredients: Trikatu, Triphala, Musta, Vida Lavana, Vidanga, Ela, Tejapatra, Trivrit, Lavanga, Sharkara.
indications: Hyperacidity (Amlapitta), Constipation (Vibandha), Piles (Arsha). Primarily a Pitta pacifier.

name: Chandraprabha Vati
ingredients: Vacha, Musta, Guduchi, Devadaru, Haridra, Ativisha, Daruharidra, Pippalimoola, Chitraka, Dhanyaka, Triphala, Chavya, Vidanga, Gaja Pippali, Trikatu, Makshika Bhasma, Yavakshara, Sarjikshara, Saindhava Lavana, Sauvarchala Lavana, Vida Lavana, Trivrit, Danti, Tejapatra, Tvak, Ela, Vamshalochana, Loha Bhasma, Sita, Shilajatu, Guggulu.
indications: Urinary tract disorders (Mutraghata), Diabetes (Prameha), Kidney stones (Ashmari), General debility.

name: Arogyavardhini Vati
ingredients: Shuddha Parada, Shuddha Gandhaka, Loha Bhasma, Abhraka Bhasma, Tamra Bhasma, Triphala, Shuddha Shilajatu, Shuddha Guggulu, Chitrakamoola, Kutaki.
indications: Liver disorders (Yakrit Vikara), Skin diseases (Kushtha), Chronic fever (Jeerna Jwara), Obesity (Sthaulya). Excellent for detox (Ama Pachana).

name: Sanjivani Vati
ingredients: Vidanga, Shunthi, Pippali, Haritaki, Amalaki, Bibhitaki, Vacha, Guduchi, Shuddha Bhallataka, Shuddha Vatsanabha.
indications: Fever (Jwara), Gastroenteritis (Visuchika), Indigestion with Ama, Snake bite and other toxic conditions.

name: Triphala Guggulu
ingredients: Haritaki, Bibhitaki, Amalaki, Pippali, Shuddha Guggulu.
indications: Joint pain with swelling (Amavata, Sandhivata), Piles (Arsha), Fistula (Bhagandara), Obesity (Sthaulya).

name: Triphala Ghrita
ingredients: Ghee processed with a decoction of Haritaki, Bibhitaki, and Amalaki.
indications: Primary medicine for all eye diseases (Netra Roga), improves vision (Chakshushya), useful for hair fall (Khalitya).

name: Brahmi Ghrita
ingredients: Ghee processed with the juice and paste of Brahmi, and powders of Vacha, Kushtha, and Shankhpushpi.
indications: Improves memory (Smriti), intellect (Dhi), and speech (Vak). Used in epilepsy (Apasmara) and insanity (Unmada).

name: Phala Ghrita
ingredients: Ghee processed with Manjistha, Madhuka, Kushtha, Triphala, Sharkara, Bala, Meda, Ashwagandha, and multiple other herbs.
indications: Female and male infertility (Vandhyatva), uterine disorders (Yoni Roga), acts as a general tonic during pregnancy.

name: Mahanarayana Taila
ingredients: A complex oil made with Bilva, Ashwagandha, Bala, Shatavari, Dashamoola group, and many other herbs processed in sesame oil, cow's milk, and Shatavari juice.
indications: All types of Vata disorders, especially Arthritis (Sandhivata), Paralysis (Pakshaghata), and nerve pain. For external massage (Abhyanga).

name: Anu Taila
ingredients: Sesame oil processed with a large number of herbs including Jivanti, Devadaru, Tvak, Musta, Shatavari, and goat's milk.
indications: For Nasya therapy. Strengthens all sense organs of the head, prevents hair fall and graying, relieves sinusitis and headache.

name: Dashamularishta
ingredients: A fermented decoction of Dashamoola (ten roots), Chitrak, Pushkarmool, Guduchi, Draksha, and other herbs.
indications: A general health tonic, used post-delivery (Sutika Roga), for Vata disorders, cough, and to improve strength and stamina.
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Arjunarishta
ingredients: A fermented decoction of Arjuna bark, Draksha (grapes), Madhuka flowers, and Dhataki flowers.
indications: Premier cardiotonic (Hridya). Used for all types of heart diseases, strengthens the heart muscle, and regulates blood pressure.
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Ashokarishta
ingredients: A fermented decoction of Ashoka bark, Dhataki, Musta, Shunthi, Daruharidra, Utpala, Haritaki, Bibhitaki, and Amalaki.
indications: A primary uterine tonic. Used for heavy menstrual bleeding (Asrigdara), painful menstruation (Kashtartava), and other female reproductive issues.
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Chyawanprash
ingredients: A complex herbal jam with Amalaki as the primary ingredient, along with Dashamoola, Ashtavarga herbs, Pippali, Vamshalochana, and many others.
indications: The foremost rejuvenative (Rasayana). Boosts immunity, strengthens the respiratory system (Kasa, Shwasa), and promotes longevity.

name: Agastya Haritaki Rasayana
ingredients: A jam-like preparation of Dashamoola, Haritaki, Pippali, Chitraka, Yava, Guda (jaggery), and other herbs.
indications: Specifically for chronic respiratory problems like asthma (Shwasa), bronchitis (Kasa), hiccups (Hikka), and tuberculosis (Kshaya).

name: Mahamanjisthadi Kwath
ingredients: A decoction of Manjistha, Musta, Kutaja, Guduchi, Kushtha, Nimba, Daruharidra, Triphala, and many other blood-purifying herbs.
indications: A potent blood purifier (Raktashodhaka). Used for all types of skin diseases (Kushtha), gout (Vatarakta), and obesity (Sthaulya).

name: Rasnairandadi Kwath
ingredients: A decoction of Rasna, Eranda, Bala, Sahachara, Shunthi, Guduchi, and other Vata-pacifying herbs.
indications: For Vata disorders, especially lower back pain (Katishula), sciatica, and arthritis (Amavata).

name: Godanti Bhasma
ingredients: A fine ash (Bhasma) prepared from purified Gypsum.
indications: Used for fever (Jwara), especially with high temperature, headache, and body aches. Also used for hyperacidity.

name: Pravala Pishti
ingredients: A fine paste (Pishti) prepared from purified Coral, processed with rose water.
indications: Excellent Pitta pacifier. Used for hyperacidity (Amlapitta), bleeding disorders (Raktapitta), and as a natural calcium supplement.

name: Yashada Bhasma
ingredients: A fine ash (Bhasma) prepared from purified Zinc.
indications: Used for eye diseases (Netra Roga), diabetes (Prameha), and as an immune booster. Also used topically in skin conditions.

name: Loha Bhasma
ingredients: A fine ash (Bhasma) prepared from purified Iron, often processed 100 or 1000 times (Shataputi/Sahasraputi).
indications: Primary remedy for iron-deficiency anemia (Pandu). It is also a powerful rejuvenative (Rasayana) that enhances strength and vitality.

name: Swarna Bhasma
ingredients: The legendary ash of purified Gold, processed with various herbal juices.
indications: The ultimate rejuvenator (Rasayana) and immunomodulator. Used as a powerful cardiotonic (Hridya), brain tonic (Medhya), and aphrodisiac (Vajikarana).

name: Kaishore Guggulu
ingredients: Guduchi, Haritaki, Bibhitaki, Amalaki, Shuddha Guggulu, Shunthi, Maricha, Pippali, Vidanga, Danti, Trivrit, Ghrita.
indications: Gout (Vatarakta), Skin diseases (Kushtha), Wounds (Vrana), Arthritis. Excellent blood purifier (Raktashodhaka).

name: Yogaraja Guggulu
ingredients: Chitraka, Pippalimoola, Yavani, Karavi, Ajamoda, Jeeraka, Suradaru, Chavya, Ela, Saindhava Lavana, Kushtha, Rasna, Gokshura, Dhanyaka, Triphala, Musta, Trikatu, Tvak, Usheera, Taleesapatra, Patra, Shuddha Guggulu.
indications: All types of Vata disorders, especially joint pain with stiffness (Amavata, Sandhivata), and neurological conditions.

name: Simhanada Guggulu
ingredients: Triphala, Shuddha Gandhaka, Shuddha Guggulu, processed with Eranda Taila (Castor Oil).
indications: Severe rheumatoid arthritis (Amavata), Gout (Vatarakta), Skin diseases (Kushtha), and respiratory conditions. Potent anti-inflammatory.

name: Gokshuradi Guggulu
ingredients: Gokshura, Shuddha Guggulu, Shunthi, Maricha, Pippali, Haritaki, Bibhitaki, Amalaki, Musta.
indications: Urinary tract disorders (Mutrakrichhra), Kidney stones (Ashmari), Diabetes (Prameha), and prostate issues.

name: Kanchanara Guggulu
ingredients: Kanchanara bark, Haritaki, Bibhitaki, Amalaki, Shunthi, Maricha, Pippali, Varuna, Ela, Tvak, Tejapatra, Shuddha Guggulu.
indications: Thyroid imbalances (Galaganda), Glandular swellings (Granthi), Tumors (Arbuda), PCOD, and other Kapha accumulations.

name: Abhayarishta
ingredients: A fermented decoction of Haritaki, Draksha (Grapes), Vidanga, Madhuka, and other herbs.
indications: Excellent laxative and carminative. Used for Piles (Arsha), Constipation (Vibandha), and bloating.
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Amritarishta
ingredients: A fermented decoction of Guduchi, Dashamoola, Jeeraka, Parpataka, Saptaparna, Shunthi, Maricha, Pippali, Musta, and other herbs.
indications: Used for all types of fevers (Jwara), especially chronic fever. It's a potent immunomodulator (Rasayana) and digestive (Deepana).
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Kutajarishta
ingredients: A fermented decoction of Kutaja bark, Draksha (Grapes), Madhuka, Gambhari, and Dhataki flowers.
indications: A specific remedy for diarrhea (Atisara), dysentery (Pravahika), Irritable Bowel Syndrome (Grahani), and bleeding piles.
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Saraswatarishta
ingredients: A fermented tonic containing Brahmi, Shatavari, Vidari, Haritaki, Ushira, Shunthi, and many other nervine herbs. A 'Swarna' version also contains gold.
indications: A famous brain tonic (Medhya Rasayana). Used to improve memory, concentration, and calm the mind. Beneficial for students and for anxiety.
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Kumaryasava
ingredients: A fermented tonic with Kumari juice, Haritaki, Loha Bhasma, Trikatu, Dhanyaka, and many other herbs.
indications: Used for liver disorders, loss of appetite, menstrual problems (amenorrhea, dysmenorrhea), and anemia.
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Vasavaleha
ingredients: A jam-like preparation of Vasa (Adhatoda vasica) juice, Pippali, Ghrita, Madhu, and Sharkara.
indications: Used for all types of cough (Kasa), asthma (Shwasa), bronchitis, and bleeding conditions like Raktapitta.

name: Chitrakaharitaki
ingredients: A formulation of Chitraka, Haritaki, Amalaki, Guduchi, Dashamoola, Trikatu, and Guda (jaggery).
indications: For chronic respiratory conditions with mucus (Kapha-dominant Kasa), chronic rhinitis (Pratishyaya), and poor digestion.

name: Mahatiktaka Ghrita
ingredients: A highly bitter medicated ghee prepared with Saptaparna, Patola, Nimba, Katuki, Guduchi, Vasa, and many other Pitta-pacifying herbs.
indications: Used for a wide range of Pitta-dominant skin diseases like psoriasis, eczema, and erysipelas. Also used in bleeding disorders and for blood purification.

name: Kalyanaka Ghrita
ingredients: Ghee processed with Vidanga, Priyangu, Triphala, Daruharidra, Manjistha, and many other herbs.
indications: A brain tonic (Medhya) used for psychological disorders (Unmada), epilepsy (Apasmara), speech problems, and to improve memory.

name: Dadimashtaka Churna
ingredients: Dadima (Pomegranate), Vamshalochana, Trikatu, Tvak, Ela, Patra, Nagakesara.
indications: Excellent for treating diarrhea (Atisara), Irritable Bowel Syndrome (Grahani), and improving digestive function.

name: Talisadi Churna
ingredients: Talisapatra, Maricha, Shunthi, Pippali, Vamshalochana, Ela, Tvak, Sharkara.
indications: A formulation for cough, cold, fever, and digestive issues. It's more heating than Sitopaladi Churna.

name: Eladi Vati
ingredients: Ela, Patra, Tvak, Pippali, Madhuka (Yashtimadhu), Kharjura (Dates), Draksha (Grapes), Madhu (Honey).
indications: A lozenge used to soothe sore throat, hoarseness of voice, cough, and bad breath.
dosage: 1-2 tablets to be chewed or sucked.

name: Khadiradi Vati
ingredients: Khadira Sara, Javitri, Kankola, Bhimseni Karpoora, Supari.
indications: An astringent tablet for mouth ulcers, sore throat, tonsillitis, and other oral cavity problems.
dosage: 1-2 tablets to be kept in the mouth and sucked.

name: Lavangadi Vati
ingredients: Lavanga (Clove), Maricha (Black Pepper), Bibhitaki, Khadira Sara.
indications: A lozenge specifically for productive cough (Kapha Kasa) and cold. It has expectorant properties.

name: Krimi Kuthar Rasa
ingredients: Shuddha Parada, Shuddha Gandhaka, Vidanga, Shuddha Vatsanabha, Ajamoda, and other herbs.
indications: A potent formulation used specifically for destroying all types of intestinal worms and parasites (Krimi).

name: Tribhuvan Kirti Rasa
ingredients: Shuddha Hingula, Shuddha Vatsanabha, Trikatu, Pippalimoola, Tankana Bhasma, processed with Tulsi, Ardraka, and Dhattura juice.
indications: A classical herbo-mineral formulation for all types of fever (Jwara), cold, cough, and body aches. It induces sweating (Swedajanana).

name: Vasant Kusumakar Rasa
ingredients: A premium formulation with Swarna Bhasma, Raupya Bhasma, Vanga Bhasma, Naga Bhasma, Loha Bhasma, Abhraka Bhasma, Pravala Bhasma, and Mukta Bhasma.
indications: A powerful rejuvenative (Rasayana), aphrodisiac (Vajikarana), and is highly beneficial in diabetes (Prameha) and its complications.

name: Shadbindu Taila
ingredients: Sesame oil processed with Eranda, Tagara, Shatahva, Jivanti, Rasna, Saindhava, Bhringaraja juice, and goat's milk.
indications: A classical oil for Nasya (nasal administration) used to treat sinusitis, headache, hair fall, and to strengthen the sense organs.

name: Jatyadi Taila
ingredients: Medicated oil prepared with Jati (Jasmine) leaves, Nimba (Neem) leaves, Patola leaves, Karanja, Madhuka, and other herbs.
indications: Renowned for its remarkable wound-healing (Vranaropana) properties. Used for all types of non-healing ulcers, burns, and skin lesions.

name: Saptamrit Lauh
ingredients: Loha Bhasma, Haritaki, Bibhitaki, Amalaki, and Madhuka (Yashtimadhu).
indications: An iron-based formulation specifically for eye (Chakshushya) and hair (Keshya) health, improving vision and preventing premature graying.

name: Mahalakshmi Vilas Rasa
ingredients: Shuddha Gandhaka, Abhraka Bhasma, Shuddha Parada, Vanga Bhasma, Tamra Bhasma, Swarna Bhasma, Raupya Bhasma, Mukta Bhasma. Processed with Bhringaraja juice and other herbs.
indications: Severe respiratory conditions (Kasa, Shwasa), Tuberculosis (Kshaya), Chronic rhinitis (Pratishyaya), and as a powerful cardiotonic (Hridya).

name: Vatagajankush Rasa
ingredients: Shuddha Parada, Shuddha Gandhaka, Loha Bhasma, Shuddha Vatsanabha, Trikatu, Haritaki, and other herbs.
indications: A powerful formulation for severe Vata disorders like paralysis (Pakshaghata), sciatica, convulsions (Akshepaka), and severe joint pain.

name: Agnitundi Vati
ingredients: Shuddha Parada, Shuddha Gandhaka, Shuddha Vatsanabha, Ajamoda, Triphala, Saindhava Lavana, Jeeraka, Shuddha Kupilu.
indications: A potent tablet used to ignite digestive fire (Agni) in cases of severe indigestion, loss of appetite, and Grahani (IBS).

name: Ichhabhedi Rasa
ingredients: Shuddha Parada, Shuddha Gandhaka, Shunthi, Maricha, Shuddha Tankana, Shuddha Jayapala Beeja.
indications: A potent purgative (Virechana) formulation, used for rapid detoxification in cases of severe constipation, ascites (Jalodara), and Ama.

name: Anandabhairava Rasa
ingredients: Shuddha Hingula, Shuddha Vatsanabha, Shunthi, Maricha, Pippali, Shuddha Tankana.
indications: Used for fever (Jwara), diarrhea (Atisara), cough (Kasa), and rheumatoid arthritis (Amavata).

name: Lasunadi Vati
ingredients: Lasuna, Shveta Jeeraka, Saindhava Lavana, Shuddha Gandhaka, Trikatu, Shuddha Hingu.
indications: Used for diarrhea (Atisara), gastroenteritis (Visuchika), indigestion, bloating, and abdominal pain.

name: Chitrakadi Vati
ingredients: Chitraka, Pippalimoola, Yavakshara, Sarjikshara, Saindhava Lavana, Sauvarchala Lavana, Vida Lavana, Samudra Lavana, Audbhida Lavana, Trikatu, Hingu, Ajamoda, Chavya.
indications: A powerful tablet to stimulate digestive fire (Agni) and digest toxins (Ama). Used for loss of appetite and indigestion.

name: Shankha Vati
ingredients: Panchalavana, Shankha Bhasma, Hingu, Chincha Kshara, Trikatu, Shuddha Vatsanabha.
indications: A fast-acting carminative for severe indigestion, hyperacidity (Amlapitta), and abdominal pain (Shula).

name: Dashamoola Kwath
ingredients: Decoction of the ten roots (Dashamoola): Bilva, Agnimantha, Shyonaka, Patala, Gambhari, Brihati, Kantakari, Shalaparni, Prishniparni, Gokshura.
indications: A premier decoction for all Vata disorders, including body aches, fever, cough, and post-partum care.

name: Varunadi Kwath
ingredients: A decoction of Varuna, Pashanabheda, Shigru, and other herbs.
indications: Used to reduce fat (Medohara), treat obesity (Sthaulya), Kapha headaches, and urinary calculi (Ashmari).

name: Drakshadi Kashayam
ingredients: A cooling decoction of Draksha (Grapes), Madhuka (Licorice), Lodhra, Sariva, and other Pitta-pacifying herbs.
indications: Used for hangovers (Madatyaya), fever with burning sensation (Pitta Jwara), vomiting, and other Pitta imbalances.

name: Gandharvahastadi Kashayam
ingredients: A decoction with Gandharvahasta (Eranda - Castor root), Shunthi, Chirabilva, and other Vata-pacifying herbs.
indications: A safe and effective laxative (Anulomana) and carminative for Vata conditions like low back pain, gas, and bloating.
anupana: With water before food, can be mixed with castor oil.

name: Narayan Churna
ingredients: Chitraka, Triphala, Trikatu, Vidanga, Trivrit, Danti, and all five types of salt (Panchalavana).
indications: Used for ascites (Jalodara), abdominal distension, constipation, and piles (Arsha).

name: Panchakola Churna
ingredients: Pippali (Long Pepper), Pippalimoola (Root of Long Pepper), Chavya, Chitraka, Shunthi (Dry Ginger).
indications: A powerful appetizer (Deepana) and digestive (Pachana). Used for loss of appetite, bloating, and spleen disorders.

name: Kushmanda Avaleha
ingredients: A nourishing jam of Kushmanda, Pippali, Ghrita, Madhu, and other strength-promoting herbs.
indications: A powerful rejuvenative (Rasayana) and aphrodisiac (Vajikarana). Used to increase strength, stamina, and weight (Brimhana).

name: Brahma Rasayana
ingredients: A powerful rejuvenative jam for the mind containing Haritaki, Amalaki, Shankhpushpi, Vacha, and many other Medhya herbs.
indications: A supreme brain tonic (Medhya Rasayana) for improving memory, intelligence, and promoting longevity. Reduces mental fatigue.

name: Vilwadi Leham
ingredients: A jam-like preparation of Bilva (Bael) root, Musta, Jeeraka, Trikatu, Ela, and other digestive herbs.
indications: Used to treat nausea, vomiting, loss of appetite, and other digestive issues. It has a carminative effect.
anupana: To be licked as is, or with honey.

name: Kottamchukkadi Thailam
ingredients: A South Indian medicated oil with Kushtha, Shunthi, Vacha, Shigru, Lasuna, and other herbs in a base of sesame oil and tamarind juice.
indications: For external application on painful joints, inflammation, and stiffness due to Vata. Relieves numbness and pain.

name: Kumkumadi Thailam
ingredients: A renowned facial oil made with Saffron, Sandalwood, Manjistha, Madhuka, Laksha, and other complexion-enhancing herbs in a sesame oil base.
indications: Improves complexion (Varnya), reduces blemishes, dark spots, and adds a natural glow to the skin.
dosage: A few drops for facial massage

name: Neelibringadi Thailam
ingredients: A famous herbal hair oil made with Nili, Bhringaraja, Amalaki, and other herbs processed in coconut oil and milk.
indications: Promotes hair growth (Keshya), prevents hair fall, and delays premature graying.

name: Sukumaram Ghritam
ingredients: A medicated ghee with Punarnava, Dashamoola, Shatavari, Eranda, Ashwagandha, and other herbs.
indications: A gentle tonic primarily for gynecological disorders, hernia, and digestive issues. It is Vata-pacifying.

name: Dadimadi Ghrita
ingredients: Medicated ghee with Pomegranate, Dhanyaka, Chitraka, and Trikatu.
indications: Used during pregnancy for nourishment (Garbhaposhaka), to treat anemia, and to correct digestive issues. It is also a heart tonic (Hridya).

name: Abhraka Bhasma
ingredients: Purified mica ash, often processed 100 (Shataputi) or 1000 (Sahasraputi) times.
indications: A potent rejuvenative (Rasayana) used for respiratory diseases, debility, and as a powerful tonic. It is tridoshic.

name: Shankha Bhasma
ingredients: Ash of purified conch shell.
indications: A rich source of calcium, used for acid peptic disorders (Amlapitta), indigestion, and malabsorption.

name: Mandura Bhasma
ingredients: Purified and processed old iron rust.
indications: An iron-rich formulation for anemia (Pandu), jaundice (Kamala), edema (Shotha), and to support liver and spleen function.

name: Smritisagar Rasa
ingredients: Shuddha Parada, Shuddha Gandhaka, Shuddha Haratala, Manashila, Tamra Bhasma, processed with Vacha, Brahmi, and Jyotishmati.
indications: A potent brain tonic (Medhya) used for improving memory (Smriti), intellect, and treating neurological conditions like epilepsy (Apasmara).

name: Ekangveer Rasa
ingredients: Shuddha Parada, Shuddha Gandhaka, Loha Bhasma, Vanga Bhasma, Abhraka Bhasma, Tamra Bhasma, Trikatu, Triphala, and many other herbs.
indications: Specifically used for treating paralysis (Pakshaghata), hemiplegia, facial palsy, and other severe Vata neurological disorders.

name: Pradarantak Lauh
ingredients: Loha Bhasma, Tamra Bhasma, Shuddha Haratala, Vanga Bhasma, Triphala, Trikatu, and other herbs.
indications: An iron-based formulation specifically used for treating excessive menstrual bleeding (menorrhagia or Pradara) and leucorrhea.

name: Navayasa Loha
ingredients: Loha Bhasma combined with nine herbs: Trikatu, Triphala, Musta, Vidanga, and Chitraka.
indications: Used to treat anemia (Pandu), jaundice (Kamala), skin diseases (Kushtha), and piles (Arsha).

name: Punarnavadi Mandura
ingredients: Mandura Bhasma, Punarnava, Trivrit, Shunthi, Pippali, Maricha, Vidanga, Devadaru, Chitraka, and many other herbs processed in Gomutra (cow's urine).
indications: A premier formulation for edema (Shotha), anemia (Pandu), spleen enlargement (Plihodara), and liver disorders.

name: Laghu Sutshekhar Rasa
ingredients: Shuddha Gairika (Red Ochre), Shunthi, processed with Nagavalli (Betel leaf) juice.
indications: A simple but effective formulation for hyperacidity (Amlapitta), headache with a burning sensation, and other Pitta-dominant conditions.

name: Sarivadyasava
ingredients: A fermented tonic of Sariva (Anantamul), Musta, Lodhra, Nyagrodha, Ashwattha, Guduchi, and many other blood-purifying herbs.
indications: A potent blood purifier (Raktashodhaka) used for skin diseases, gout (Vatarakta), syphilis, and boils.
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Punarnavasava
ingredients: A fermented tonic of Punarnava, Gokshura, Kantakari, Vasa, Guduchi, and other diuretic and anti-inflammatory herbs.
indications: A powerful diuretic and liver tonic, used for edema (Shotha), ascites (Jalodara), and kidney or liver disorders.
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Drakshasava
ingredients: A fermented preparation of Draksha, Tvak, Ela, Patra, Nagakesara, Priyangu, Maricha, Pippali, and other herbs.
indications: A general tonic used for improving digestion and strength, especially in cases of debility, indigestion, and piles (Arsha).
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Lohasava
ingredients: An iron-rich fermented tonic made with processed Loha Bhasma, Triphala, Trikatu, Vidanga, Musta, Chitraka, and other herbs.
indications: Used for iron-deficiency anemia (Pandu), swelling (Shotha), and liver and spleen enlargement.
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Pippalyasava
ingredients: A fermented tonic with Pippali, Maricha, Chavya, Haridra, Chitraka, Musta, Vidanga, Triphala, and other herbs.
indications: Used for malabsorption syndrome (Grahani), digestive issues, abdominal tumors (Gulma), and respiratory ailments.
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Pathyadi Kwath
ingredients: A decoction of Haritaki, Bibhitaki, Amalaki, Bhunimba (Kalmegh), Nimba, Guduchi, and Haridra.
indications: Specifically used for all types of headaches (Shirashula), especially migraines, sinus headaches, and tension headaches.

name: Maharasnadi Kwath
ingredients: A complex decoction with Rasna, Dhanvayasa, Bala, Eranda Moola, Devadaru, Vacha, Shunthi, Guduchi, and many other Vata-pacifying herbs.
indications: A highly effective formulation for all types of Vata disorders, including paralysis, sciatica, and chronic arthritis.

name: Panchatikta Ghrita Guggulu
ingredients: A combination of five bitter herbs (Nimba, Patola, Guduchi, Vasa, Kantakari) with Triphala, Guggulu, and processed in Ghrita.
indications: Used for deep-seated inflammation, bone and joint disorders, skin diseases, and non-healing ulcers.

name: Amritottaram Kashayam
ingredients: A simple but effective decoction of Guduchi, Shunthi (Dry Ginger), and Haritaki.
indications: Used for all types of fevers (Jwara), especially with digestive complaints. It digests Ama (Amapachana) and acts as a mild laxative.

name: Kushmanda Rasayana
ingredients: A nourishing jam of Kushmanda (Ash Gourd), Pippali, Ghrita, Madhu, and other strength-promoting herbs. Often used interchangeably with Kushmanda Avaleha.
indications: A powerful rejuvenative (Rasayana) and aphrodisiac (Vajikarana). Used to increase strength, stamina, and weight (Brimhana).

name: Manibhadra Guda
ingredients: A jaggery-based formulation with Vidanga, Amalaki, Haritaki, Trivrit, and other herbs.
indications: Used as a laxative and for treating skin diseases (Kushtha), intestinal worms (Krimi), and piles (Arsha).

name: Narikel Lavan
ingredients: A special preparation made by filling a coconut with rock salt and processing it in fire.
indications: An excellent remedy for hyperacidity (Amlapitta), abdominal pain, and peptic ulcers.

name: Panchagavya Ghrita
ingredients: A medicated ghee made from the five products of the cow: milk, curd, ghee, urine, and dung.
indications: Used for neurological and psychological disorders like epilepsy (Apasmara), insanity (Unmada), and fever (Jwara).

name: Dhanwantaram Gulika
ingredients: A classical tablet containing Ela, Shunthi, Haritaki, Jeeraka, Chandana, Bhunimba, and other herbs, processed in Jeeraka decoction.
indications: Used for respiratory issues like cough, breathlessness, and hiccups. Also beneficial for digestive discomfort and nausea.
anupana: With warm water or ginger juice.

name: Vettumaran Gulika
ingredients: A potent tablet with Shuddha Hingula, Shuddha Vatsanabha, Maricha (Black Pepper), Pippali, and other heating herbs, processed in ginger juice.
indications: A fast-acting diaphoretic (induces sweating) used in the early stages of fever, especially with chills, body aches, and indigestion.
anupana: With ginger juice or hot water.

name: Pushyanuga Churna
ingredients: A complex powder with Patha, Jambu-bija, Amra-bija, Lodhra, Manjistha, Arjuna, and many other astringent herbs.
indications: A classical formulation used for treating all types of gynecological bleeding disorders (Asrigdara, Raktapradara) and leucorrhea (Shveta Pradara).

name: Gangadhara Churna
ingredients: A classical powder used specifically for diarrhea and dysentery. Two versions exist: one with Musta, Arjuna, Lodhra; another with Musta, Kutaja, Bilva.
indications: Used for all types of diarrhea (Atisara) and dysentery (Pravahika). It is an excellent astringent (Grahi).

name: Ajamodadi Churna
ingredients: Ajamoda, Vidanga, Devadaru, Chitraka, Pippalimoola, Saindhava Lavana, and other Vata-Kapha pacifying herbs.
indications: A specific remedy for joint pain, rheumatoid arthritis (Amavata), sciatica, and other inflammatory joint conditions.

name: Sudarshan Churna
ingredients: A simpler version of Mahasudarshan Churna, with Chirayata as the main ingredient, combined with Guduchi, Triphala, Haridra, and other bitter herbs.
indications: A bitter powder used effectively for all types of fever (Jwara), especially those associated with liver dysfunction. It is a potent Ama-pachaka.

name: Dhanwantaram Thailam
ingredients: A complex Vata-pacifying oil with Bala, Yava, Kola, Kulattha, Dashamoola, and many other herbs processed in sesame oil and cow's milk.
indications: A versatile oil for Vata conditions, widely used pre and post-natal care, for arthritis, paralysis, and neurological issues.

name: Kshirabala Thailam
ingredients: A nourishing and cooling oil made with Bala (Sida cordifolia), cow's milk, and sesame oil. Often processed multiple times (Avarti) for increased potency.
indications: Excellent for neurological Vata disorders, insomnia, facial paralysis, spondylosis, and pain with burning sensation. It's a potent rejuvenative (Rasayana).
dosage: For external use; also used for Nasya & Basti

name: Lakshadi Thailam
ingredients: A nourishing oil prepared with Laksha (lac), Ashwagandha, Haridra, Musta, and other herbs in a base of sesame oil and curd.
indications: A strengthening and nourishing massage oil, especially recommended for babies, children, and convalescing adults to build muscle mass and strength (Balya).

name: Murivenna
ingredients: 'Wound Oil' made with Karanja leaves, Tambula (Betel leaf), Shigru leaves, Kumari (Aloe Vera), and other herbs in a coconut oil base.
indications: A famous first-aid remedy for acute injuries like cuts, burns, sprains, cramps, and fractures. It promotes wound healing (Vranaropana) and relieves pain.

name: Pinda Thailam
ingredients: A cooling oil made with Madhucchishta (Beeswax), Manjistha, Sariva, and Sarjarasa in a sesame oil base.
indications: Used topically to relieve the pain and burning sensation (Daha) of gout (Vatarakta) and Pitta-type arthritis.

name: Sukumaram Kashayam
ingredients: A decoction form of Sukumaram Ghritam, containing Punarnava, Dashamoola, Shatavari, Eranda, Ashwagandha, and other herbs.
indications: A gentle tonic (Sukumara) for gynecological issues like PCOD, hernia, and digestive disorders like bloating and constipation.

name: Varanadi Kashayam
ingredients: A decoction of Varuna, Shatavari, Chitraka, Shigru, Bilva, and other Kapha-pacifying herbs.
indications: Reduces fat (Medohara), treats obesity (Sthaulya), Kapha-type headaches, internal abscesses, and urinary calculi.

name: Dhanadanayanadi Kashayam
ingredients: A classical decoction used for Vata disorders like facial paralysis (Ardita), tremors, and hemiplegia.
indications: Effective in treating neurological conditions caused by Vata, especially facial paralysis and tremors.

name: Guluchyadi Kashayam
ingredients: A decoction with Guduchi, Dhanyaka (Coriander), Nimba (Neem), Rakta Chandana, and Padmaka.
indications: A potent remedy for Pitta-dominant fevers (Jwara), burning sensation (Daha), vomiting, and excessive thirst (Trishna).

name: Elakanadi Kashayam
ingredients: A classical decoction containing Ela (Cardamom), Pippali, Shunthi, Vasa, Guduchi, and other herbs.
indications: Used for chronic respiratory conditions like asthma (Shwasa), bronchitis (Kasa), and tuberculosis (Kshaya), especially when Kapha is dominant.

name: Rajapravartini Vati
ingredients: Kanyasara (Aloe Vera extract), Shuddha Tankana, Shuddha Hingu, Kasis Bhasma.
indications: A formulation used to induce and regulate menstrual flow in cases of amenorrhea (loss of menses) and dysmenorrhea (painful menses).

name: Krimi Mudgar Rasa
ingredients: Shuddha Parada, Shuddha Gandhaka, Vidanga, Palasha bija, Ajamoda, Shuddha Kupilu.
indications: A potent formulation for destroying and expelling all types of intestinal worms and parasites (Krimi).

name: Nityananda Rasa
ingredients: A complex formula with Shuddha Hingula, Vanga Bhasma, Kanta Loha Bhasma, Tamra Bhasma, Triphala, Trikatu, and many other herbs.
indications: A classical formulation used for treating filariasis (elephantiasis or Shlipada), tumors, and other glandular swellings.

name: Swarna Vasant Malti Rasa
ingredients: Swarna Bhasma, Mukta Bhasma, Shuddha Hingula, Maricha (Black Pepper), Yashada Bhasma, processed in butter and lemon juice.
indications: A precious formulation used as a powerful immunomodulator for chronic fevers, tuberculosis (Kshaya), debility, and as a general rejuvenative.

name: Makaradhwaj Vati
ingredients: Siddha Makaradhwaja (a sublimate of gold, mercury, and sulfur), Swarna Bhasma, Jatiphala (Nutmeg), Lavanga, and other herbs.
indications: A famous aphrodisiac and rejuvenative formulation, used to enhance vitality, strength, and sexual wellness.

name: Swarna Makshika Bhasma
ingredients: Ash of purified Chalcopyrite (an ore of copper and iron).
indications: Used for anemia (Pandu), jaundice (Kamala), diabetes (Prameha), and as a rejuvenative that pacifies Pitta.

name: Hajrul Yahood Bhasma
ingredients: Ash of a fossilized stone (a type of lime silicate).
indications: A potent diuretic and lithotriptic (stone-breaking), used for kidney and bladder stones (Ashmari) and painful urination (Mutrakrichhra).
anupana: With coconut water or Varunadi Kwath.

name: Kaharuba Pishti
ingredients: A fine powder (Pishti) of purified Amber processed with rose water.
indications: A powerful hemostatic (stops bleeding), used for bleeding piles, menorrhagia, ulcers, and as a cardiac tonic.

name: Akika Pishti
ingredients: A fine powder (Pishti) of purified Agate gem processed with various herbal juices.
indications: A potent cardiotonic (Hridya) and Pitta pacifier, used for heart health, palpitations, and bleeding disorders.

name: Dhatupaushtik Churna
ingredients: A nutritive powder with Shatavari, Gokshura, Bala, Ashwagandha, Shveta Musli, and many other aphrodisiac and tonic herbs.
indications: Nourishes all body tissues (dhatus) and is used as a powerful aphrodisiac (Vajikarana), strength-promoter (Balya), and rejuvenative.

name: Haridra Khanda
ingredients: A granular preparation of Haridra (Turmeric), Trikatu, Triphala, Trivrit, and other herbs processed with milk, ghee, and sugar.
indications: A famous remedy for all types of skin allergies, hives (Urticaria), itching (Kandu), and allergic rhinitis.

name: Drakshavaleha
ingredients: A jam-like preparation of Draksha, Pippali, Madhuka (Licorice), Sharkara, and other herbs.
indications: Used to treat anemia (Pandu), jaundice (Kamala), hyperacidity, and as a nourishing tonic to improve strength and digestion.

name: Maha Sudarshan Churna
ingredients: A highly bitter powder of over 50 herbs, with Chirayata (Kalmegh) as the main ingredient, along with Guduchi, Triphala, Haridra, Musta, and many others.
indications: One of the foremost remedies for all types of fevers (Jwara), especially chronic and intermittent fevers. It is a potent detoxifier for the liver and blood.

name: Bilvadi Gutika
ingredients: A tablet made from Bilva root, Surasa, Karanja, Trikatu, and processed in Gomutra (cow's urine).
indications: Used for gastroenteritis (Visuchika), insect bites, and conditions believed to be caused by toxins (Visha).

name: Sameer Pannag Rasa
ingredients: A potent herbo-mineral formulation made by the sublimation of Shuddha Parada, Gandhaka, Haratala, and Manashila, processed with Aloe Vera juice.
indications: Used for respiratory and neurological conditions with severe pain and stiffness, such as asthma and rheumatoid arthritis. It acts as a bronchodilator.
anupana: With honey and betel leaf juice.

name: Vatavidhwansan Rasa
ingredients: Shuddha Parada, Shuddha Gandhaka, Naga Bhasma, Vanga Bhasma, Loha Bhasma, Tamra Bhasma, Abhraka Bhasma, Shuddha Vatsanabha, Trikatu, and many other herbs.
indications: A potent formulation for severe Vata disorders like neuritis, neuralgia, paralysis, and abdominal colic. It is a strong analgesic.

name: Pratap Lankeshwar Rasa
ingredients: Shuddha Parada, Shuddha Gandhaka, Abhraka Bhasma, Loha Bhasma, Raupya Bhasma, processed with Kumari (Aloe Vera) juice.
indications: Used as a puerperal tonic (for post-delivery care), and for intermittent fevers, cough, and digestive weakness.

name: Rasamanikya
ingredients: A jewel-like preparation made from purified Haratala (Orpiment).
indications: A potent medicine for skin diseases like psoriasis, eczema, leucoderma, and other chronic conditions. Also used in respiratory ailments.

name: Hinguleshwar Rasa
ingredients: Shuddha Hingula, Pippali, and Shuddha Vatsanabha.
indications: Used for treating fever (Jwara) and body aches, especially fevers that are difficult to manage.

name: Krimi Mudgar Rasa
ingredients: Shuddha Parada, Shuddha Gandhaka, Vidanga, Palasha bija, Ajamoda, Shuddha Kupilu.
indications: A potent formulation for destroying and expelling all types of intestinal worms and parasites (Krimi).

name: Karpasasthyadi Thailam
ingredients: Medicated oil with Karpasa Asthi, Bala, Masha, Devadaru, Rasna, and other herbs, processed in sesame oil and goat's milk.
indications: Used for Vata disorders like paralysis, facial palsy (Ardita), spondylosis, and other neurological conditions.

name: Balaswagandhadi Thailam
ingredients: A highly nourishing oil combining Bala, Ashwagandha, Laksha, and other herbs in a sesame oil base.
indications: Used to build muscle mass, strengthen nerves, and for rejuvenation after debilitating diseases. Excellent for general body massage.

name: Sahacharadi Thailam
ingredients: A medicated oil with Sahachara, Dashamoola, Manjistha, and other herbs, processed in sesame oil and milk.
indications: Primarily used for Vata disorders affecting the lower limbs, such as sciatica, varicose veins, and muscle stiffness.

name: Eladi Thailam
ingredients: An aromatic oil with Ela, Kushtha, Tagara, Jati, Kumkuma (Saffron), and other herbs in a sesame or coconut oil base.
indications: Used for skin detoxification, improving complexion (Varnya), and treating skin issues like allergic dermatitis, itching, and scabies.

name: Nalpamaradi Thailam
ingredients: An oil made with the four Ficus barks (Nalpamara), Haridra (Turmeric), Chandana (Sandalwood), Ushira, and other cooling herbs.
indications: A famous skin brightening and de-tanning oil. Used for improving complexion and treating minor skin irritations and sun damage.

name: Patolamuladi Kwath
ingredients: A bitter decoction of Patola (Pointed Gourd root), Triphala, Guduchi, Katuki, and other herbs.
indications: Used for a variety of skin diseases (Kushtha), fevers, liver issues, and toxic conditions. Excellent Pitta pacifier.

name: Phalatrikadi Kwath
ingredients: A decoction of Triphala (Phalatrika), Amrita, Vasa, and other herbs.
indications: Used for vomiting (Chardi), hyperacidity (Amlapitta), anorexia, and other digestive issues with a Pitta predominance.

name: Brihatyadi Kashayam
ingredients: A decoction including the two types of Brihati, Prishniparni, Shalaparni, and Gokshura.
indications: Used for urinary tract disorders like painful urination (dysuria or Mutrakrichhra) and cystitis.

name: Indukantham Kashayam
ingredients: A decoction containing Dashamoola, Pippali, Chavya, Chitraka, and other herbs. It is the decoction form of Indukantham Ghrita.
indications: Used for enhancing immunity, treating chronic fevers, debility, and Vata disorders, especially of the abdomen.

name: Vidangarishta
ingredients: A fermented tonic with Vidanga, Pippalimoola, Rasna, Kutaja, Triphala, and other herbs.
indications: The foremost remedy for all types of intestinal worms and parasites (Krimi). Also used for fistula and abdominal tumors.
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Rohitakarishta
ingredients: A fermented formulation of Rohitaka bark, Triphala, Trikatu, Chitraka, and other digestive herbs.
indications: Used primarily for liver and spleen enlargement (Plihayakrid Roga), ascites, and other related digestive disorders.
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Jeerakarishtam
ingredients: A fermented tonic with Jeeraka, Dhataki, Shunthi, Jatiphala, Tvak, Ela, and other herbs.
indications: Specifically used for post-natal care (Sutika) to improve digestion, restore strength, and promote lactation. Also used for chronic indigestion.
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Guggulutiktakam Ghritam
ingredients: A bitter medicated ghee with Guggulu, Nimba, Patola, Vasa, Guduchi, and other herbs.
indications: Used for chronic skin diseases (Kushtha), arthritis (Vatarakta, Sandhivata), and deep-seated inflammation.

name: Drakshadi Leham
ingredients: A jam-like preparation of Draksha, Pippali, Madhuka (Licorice), Sharkara, and other herbs. Same as Drakshavaleha.
indications: Used to treat anemia (Pandu), jaundice (Kamala), hyperacidity, and as a nourishing tonic to improve strength and digestion.

name: Madhusnuhi Rasayana
ingredients: A rejuvenative formulation with Madhusnuhi (Chopchini), Guggulu, Triphala, Trikatu, Vidanga, and other herbs in a base of ghee and jaggery.
indications: Used for chronic skin diseases, diabetes (Prameha), and as a powerful detoxifier and rejuvenative.

name: Mahasudarshan Churna
ingredients: A highly bitter powder of over 50 herbs, with Chirayata (Kalmegh) as the main ingredient, along with Guduchi, Triphala, Haridra, Musta, and many others.
indications: One of the foremost remedies for all types of fevers (Jwara), especially chronic and intermittent fevers. It is a potent detoxifier for the liver and blood.

name: Vyoshadi Vati
ingredients: A pungent tablet containing Trikatu, Talisapatra, Chitraka, Jeeraka, and other Kapha-pacifying herbs.
indications: Used for cough, cold, rhinitis (Pratishyaya), and to clear sinus congestion. It stimulates appetite.
anupana: To be chewed or taken with warm water.

name: Marichyadi Vati
ingredients: A pungent tablet with Maricha (Black Pepper), Pippali, Yavakshara, and Dadima (Pomegranate) skin.
indications: Very effective in clearing productive cough (Kapha Kasa) and congestion in the throat and chest.
anupana: To be chewed or taken with warm water.

name: Gandhak Rasayan
ingredients: A formulation of purified sulfur (Gandhaka) processed multiple times with various herbal juices like Guduchi, Triphala, and Bhringaraja.
indications: A potent medicine for skin diseases (Kushtha), allergies, itching (Kandu), and blood purification. It is also a rejuvenative (Rasayana).

name: Dhanwantaram Kashayam
ingredients: A decoction form of Dhanwantaram Thailam, used internally for Vata disorders, especially post-delivery care, hernia, and arthritis.
indications: Relieves pain and inflammation in Vata disorders, strengthens nerves, and is a tonic for women after childbirth.

name: Brihat Vata Chintamani Rasa
ingredients: Swarna Bhasma, Raupya Bhasma, Abhraka Bhasma, Loha Bhasma, Pravala Bhasma, Mukta Bhasma, Rasa Sindura, processed with Kumari (Aloe Vera) juice.
indications: A supreme rejuvenative for Vata disorders. Used for paralysis, hemiplegia, psychosis, and as a potent cardiotonic and anti-aging medicine.

name: Kamadugha Rasa (with Mukta)
ingredients: Pravala Pishti, Guduchi Satva, Shankha Bhasma, Kapardika Bhasma, Shuddha Gairika, and Mukta Pishti (Pearl).
indications: An excellent Pitta pacifier. Used for hyperacidity (Amlapitta), ulcers, bleeding disorders (Raktapitta), and burning sensations (Daha).

name: Sutshekhar Rasa
ingredients: Shuddha Parada, Swarna Bhasma, Shuddha Gandhaka, Tankana Bhasma, Vatsanabha, Trikatu, Dhattura, and other herbs.
indications: Used for hyperacidity with vomiting, headache/migraine, asthma, and other Pitta-dominant conditions.

name: Navaratna Raja Mriganka Rasa
ingredients: A complex formulation with Bhasmas of Gold, Diamond, Pearl, and other precious gems (Navaratna), along with herbo-mineral ingredients.
indications: Used for chronic Vata diseases, tuberculosis (Rajayakshma), and as a powerful rejuvenative and aphrodisiac for severe debility.

name: Tamra Bhasma
ingredients: Ash of purified Copper, processed with various herbal juices.
indications: A potent medicine for liver and spleen disorders, ascites (Jalodara), obesity, and hyperacidity. It is extremely heating and sharp.

name: Vanga Bhasma
ingredients: Ash of purified Tin, processed with various herbal juices.
indications: Used in Ayurveda for urinary tract disorders, diabetes (Prameha), and as an aphrodisiac, especially for premature ejaculation.

name: Naga Bhasma
ingredients: Ash of purified Lead, processed with various herbal juices.
indications: Used in minute doses for diabetes, skin diseases, and chronic digestive issues like Grahani (IBS). Must be used with extreme caution.

name: Shukti Bhasma
ingredients: Ash of purified Oyster shell.
indications: A natural calcium source used for hyperacidity, ulcers, and digestive colic. It is a potent antacid (Amlapittahara).
anupana: With lemon juice or warm water.

name: Kapardika Bhasma
ingredients: Ash of purified Cowrie shell.
indications: Excellent for digestive issues like colic, malabsorption (Grahani), and bloating. It is a fast-acting carminative.

name: Dhatri Loha
ingredients: Loha Bhasma, Amalaki juice, Madhuka (Licorice).
indications: An iron formulation used for anemia (Pandu), jaundice (Kamala), and especially for hyperacidity (Amlapitta).

name: Tapyadi Loha
ingredients: Swarna Makshika Bhasma, Raupya Bhasma, Loha Bhasma, Mandura Bhasma, and many other herbo-mineral ingredients.
indications: Used for anemia, liver conditions (jaundice), and as a general health tonic. It is tridoshic.

name: Balarishta
ingredients: A fermented tonic with Bala, Ashwagandha, Eranda, and other Vata-pacifying herbs.
indications: A powerful nerve tonic used to treat Vata disorders, general debility, joint pain, and to promote strength (Balya).
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Vidaryadyasava
ingredients: A fermented tonic with Vidari, Eranda, Musli, Gokshura, and many other nourishing herbs.
indications: A cardiac tonic and general health promoter used for emaciation, debility, and as a rejuvenative (Rasayana).
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Patrangasava
ingredients: A fermented tonic with Patranga (Sappanwood), Khadira, Vasa, Lodhra, Manjistha, and other astringent herbs.
indications: Used as a uterine astringent for heavy menstrual bleeding (menorrhagia) and leucorrhea (Shveta Pradara).
anupana: With an equal quantity of water after meals.

name: Varanadi Ghritam
ingredients: A medicated ghee containing the herbs of the Varanadi group, such as Varuna, Shatavari, Chitraka, and Shigru.
indications: A scraping (Lekhana) ghee used for obesity (Sthaulya), high cholesterol, internal abscesses, and Kapha headaches.

name: Indukantham Ghritam
ingredients: Medicated ghee of the Indukantham Kashayam herbs, including Dashamoola, Pippali, Chavya, and Chitraka.
indications: Used for enhancing immunity, treating chronic fevers, debility, and Vata disorders of the abdomen.

name: Mahatriphala Ghrita
ingredients: A potent version of Triphala Ghrita, processed with the juices of Bhringaraja, Vasa, Guduchi, and other herbs.
indications: An excellent medicine for a wide range of eye diseases (Netra Roga), including night blindness and myopia. It improves vision.

name: Dashamoola Haritaki Leham
ingredients: A jam-like preparation of Dashamoola decoction, Haritaki, Trikatu, Guda (jaggery), and other herbs.
indications: Used for chronic inflammatory conditions, swelling (Shotha), Vata disorders, and loss of appetite.

name: Asanavilwadi Thailam
ingredients: A medicated oil with Asana, Vilwa, Bala, Guduchi, and other herbs, processed in sesame oil and milk.
indications: Primarily for diseases of the head, eyes, and ears. Used for headache, eye pain, and earache.
dosage: For head massage or local application

name: Balahatadi Thailam
ingredients: A cooling oil made with Bala, Amalaki, Guduchi, Chandana, and other Pitta-pacifying herbs.
indications: Used for head massage to treat Pitta-dominant headaches, burning sensation in the eyes and head, and to promote sleep.

name: Karpooradi Thailam
ingredients: A simple yet effective oil with Karpoora and Ajamoda processed in a base of coconut or sesame oil.
indications: Used externally to relieve muscle cramps, stiffness, joint pain, and chest congestion. It is fast-acting.

name: Patolakaturohinyadi Kashayam
ingredients: A bitter decoction of Patola, Katuki, Chandana, Guduchi, Musta, and Patha.
indications: Used for skin diseases with burning and itching, fevers, and toxic conditions. It is a powerful Pitta pacifier and detoxifier.

name: Punarnavadi Kashayam
ingredients: A decoction of Punarnava, Daruharidra, Haritaki, Guduchi, and other diuretic herbs.
indications: Diuretic and anti-inflammatory. Advised in Fever (Jvara), Cough (Kasa), generalized inflammatory changes/swelling (Shotha / Shopha / Edema), anemia, and ascites.

name: Guggulutiktakam Kashayam
ingredients: A decoction form of Guggulutiktakam Ghritam, containing Guggulu and a group of bitter herbs like Nimba, Patola, and Vasa.
indications: Used for chronic inflammatory conditions of the bones and joints, arthritis, and deep-seated skin diseases.

name: Rasnasaptakam Kashayam
ingredients: A decoction of seven herbs led by Rasna, including Guduchi, Eranda Moola, Devadaru, and Shunthi.
indications: Used for Vata disorders affecting the hips, back, and legs, such as low back pain, sciatica, and pain in the hip region.

name: Vyaghryadi Kashayam
ingredients: Vyaghri/Kantakari (Solanum xanthocarpum), Shunthi (Zingiber officinale), Pippali (Piper longum).
indications: A specialized decoction advised in Fever (Jvara / Santapa) and Cough (Kasa). Highly suitable for patients presenting with upper respiratory tract infections like common cold (Pinasa / Pratishyaya) and breathlessness (Shwasa).
differentiation_note: Vyaghryadi is hot and dry, making it better suited than Punarnavadi for patients with common cold (Pinasa) and runny nose, whereas Punarnavadi is appropriate for generalized inflammatory swelling (Shotha).

name: Jatyadi Ghrita
ingredients: Jatipatra (Jasmine leaves), Nimba (Neem), Patola (Pointed gourd), Naktamala/Karanja (Pongamia pinnata), Siktha (Beeswax), Madhuka (Licorice), Kushtha (Saussurea lappa), Haridra (Turmeric), Daruharidra (Tree turmeric), Katuki (Picrorhiza kurroa), Manjistha (Rubia cordifolia), Abhaya (Vetiveria zizanioides / Usheera in this context), Sariva (Hemidesmus indicus), Tuttha (Copper sulfate) processed in Ghrita (medicated ghee).
indications: External application for cleansing and healing chronic ulcers (Dushta Vrana), non-healing wounds, burn wounds, and diabetic wounds.
dosage: For external application on affected wounds only

ambiguity_notes.Abhaya: In the context of Jatyadi Ghrita, 'Abhaya' refers to Vetiveria zizanioides (Vetiver/Usheera) rather than the common Terminalia chebula (Haritaki) because of its cooling and healing properties.
